title: PM Mudra Yojana
meta_description: PM Mudra Yojana provides collateral-free loans up to ₹10 lakh for small businesses. Check loan types (Shishu, Kishore, Tarun), eligibility, and how to apply.

hero.one_liner: Collateral-free loans up to ₹10 lakh for micro and small businesses through banks, NBFCs, and MFIs

what_is_it: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY) was launched on 8 April 2015 to provide institutional credit to micro and small businesses. The scheme offers collateral-free loans up to ₹10 lakh to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises. Loans are available through all commercial banks, RRBs, small finance banks, MFIs, and NBFCs.

The scheme has three categories based on loan amount: Shishu (up to ₹50,000) for businesses just starting out, Kishore (₹50,001 to ₹5 lakh) for growing businesses, and Tarun (₹5,00,001 to ₹10 lakh) for established businesses looking to expand. No collateral or guarantee is needed for any category. Over 47 crore loans totaling ₹27+ lakh crore have been disbursed since launch.

eligibility.label: Who can apply
eligibility.value: Any Indian citizen with a business plan for non-farm income-generating activity

eligibility.label: Business types
eligibility.value: Manufacturing, trading, services, food processing, textile, transport, etc.

eligibility.label: Collateral
eligibility.value: No collateral or third-party guarantee required

eligibility.label: Documents
eligibility.value: Aadhaar, PAN, business proof, 2 photos, bank statements, business plan (for larger loans)

how_to_apply.title: Prepare a basic business plan
how_to_apply.description: For Shishu loans (up to ₹50K), a verbal explanation often suffices. For Kishore and Tarun, prepare a one-page plan: what you'll do, how much you need, and how you'll repay.

how_to_apply.title: Visit any bank branch
how_to_apply.description: Go to your nearest bank (any nationalized bank, SBI, or private bank). Ask for a Mudra loan application form. You can also apply through the Mudra portal online.

how_to_apply.title: Submit documents
how_to_apply.description: Submit identity proof (Aadhaar), address proof, business proof (if existing), quotation of items to be purchased, and passport photos.

how_to_apply.title: Loan processing
how_to_apply.description: Bank evaluates your application. Shishu loans are typically processed within 7-10 days. Kishore and Tarun may take 2-4 weeks. Once approved, amount is disbursed to your account.

important_alert.text: Banks cannot reject Mudra loan applications without valid reason. If rejected unfairly, you can file a complaint on the MUDRA portal or contact the banking ombudsman. Many banks have specific Mudra loan desks — ask for them.

important_dates.label: Application
important_dates.value: Open year-round at all banks and MFIs

important_dates.label: Mudra Card
important_dates.value: Available for Shishu loans — works like a credit card for business expenses

faqs.question: Do I need to have an existing business to get a Mudra loan?
faqs.answer: No. You can apply for a Shishu loan even for a new business you want to start. You just need to explain what business you plan to do and how you'll use the money.

faqs.question: What is the interest rate on Mudra loans?
faqs.answer: There is no fixed rate. Interest rates vary by bank, typically 8-12% per annum for Mudra loans. Shishu loans often have lower rates. Compare rates across banks before applying.

faqs.question: Can I get a Mudra loan if I have a low CIBIL score?
faqs.answer: Mudra loans, especially Shishu category, don't strictly require a high CIBIL score. However, having a very poor score or outstanding defaults may lead to rejection. Shishu loans under ₹50K are the easiest to get.

faqs.question: What's the repayment period?
faqs.answer: Typically 3-5 years for Shishu and Kishore, up to 7 years for Tarun loans. The exact tenure depends on your bank and business type. EMIs start after a moratorium period (usually 6 months).
